business_ideas.idea: Translate 1000 classic books into dozens of languages with AI and create audiobooks
K-12 school inspired by ancient Greece emphasizing reasoning, logic, mathematics, writing
Monuments project: build large-scale inspiring sculptures and beauty in cities

predictions.text: AI will largely be business as usual; certain teams will shrink while others won't
Two-person billion-dollar AI companies are overstated in medium term
Customer success will see most AI impact and team shrinkage early

dynamics: Elad drives with philosophical depth on investing, markets, and societal impact. Sam and Shaan ask clarifying questions and riff on tangents (cynicism in America, travel philosophy, Jiu-Jitsu community). Elad rarely reveals quick answers; prefers nuanced thinking. High intellectual engagement with personal anecdotes about travel, family, and learning. Tangent-heavy but substantive throughout.
frameworks: Market-first vs founder-first investing; market can crush great founder or elevate mediocre ones
The four levels of luck: blind luck, prepared mind luck, luck from motion, luck from reputation
Missionary vs mercenary vs artist: career stages require different motivations over time
One critical thing matters in most businesses; executives often overthink 5 when 1-2 drive success
Aging as developmental program (not just damage) means lifespan can be extended via drugs
Technology as lever on the world; societal impact precedes financial returns
Intensive immersive travel: spend 1+ month in one place, learn skill, join community
learnings: Great markets can make mediocre founders succeed; terrible markets crush exceptional founders
The best investors focus on market structure, growth rates, technology shifts, not hype
Defense tech is opportunity when big tech companies retreat (Google shuttering Maven)
Community-based learning (Jiu-Jitsu, yoga, pastry-making) creates lasting bonds across language barriers
Building inspiring monuments reflects societal optimism; their absence reflects cynicism creep
Aging science dramatically underinvested due to FDA not classifying aging as disease
Most investors are money-driven; the best are mission-driven, which paradoxically creates better returns

banger_quotes.quote: When a great entrepreneur meets a bad market, only one keeps their reputation.
Follow technology first or market change first; founders will surprise you.
Most businesses have one or two things that matter; everything else is noise.
The closest thing we have to monuments now is Tesla and SpaceX.
It only takes one person to spark inspiration for everyone else.

numbers_dropped: Elad invested in 40+ unicorns at early stages (seed to Series A)
YC first decade: $12M invested turned into $10B value (~320x with dilution)
BioAge IPO: new drug for muscle in clinical trials
Rapamycin: extends lifespan 10-30% in mice depending on sex
Gene knockouts: can extend organism lifespan 2-3x in healthy adults
Aging research: dramatically underfunded vs cancer research at NIH
Elad deployed >50% of personal wealth into private companies
SpaceX check minimum: was $500M+ when Elad couldn't participate
notable: Elad's philosophy is unusually mission-driven for a billionaire investor; emphasizes technology as lever for society over financial returns; life advice about intensive travel and community immersion unexpectedly useful to Sam
